source: Manual Chromium review plus Playwright accessibility compatibility suite

manual_review.method: Live local server sessions driven with real Tab and workspace keyboard input, accessibility-tree inspection, geometry checks, and screenshots.
observed_interactions: Product Frame tabs retain focus; Inspect focuses Back; Back restores the originating Inspect control; state and responsive controls retain focus.
Architecture mode, topology camera, focus targets, and keyboard-only annotation remain operable; annotation returns focus to Targeted note and updates a polite live region.
Architecture exposes one named graph application inside one named viewport region and removes 520 node and edge elements from the sequential Tab order.
Research filtering remains keyboard operable and retains focus.
Business reasoning remains readable with named, non-color-only actor and outcome states.
Review Acceptance Criterion tabs and tree Left, Right, parent, Home, End, typeahead, and activation behavior retain focus and selected state.

remediated_findings: Added the missing Product comparison heading level and explicit Inspect/Back focus handoff.
Named the Architecture inspector and Review navigator complementary landmarks.
Made Pending feedback a polite additions region and returned focus to the note input.
Replaced the large graph's 520 direct node and edge Tab stops with bounded keyboard controls while retaining pointer selection and Component targeting.
Removed nested application boundaries and named the remaining Architecture graph application.
Kept Review source status labels unbroken at 320 CSS pixels.
residual_risks: Manual browser review covered Chromium only; Firefox and WebKit were not manually exercised.
The 320 CSS-pixel check emulates 400-percent reflow and is not a native browser zoom session.
No real screen-reader speech, forced-colors or high-contrast mode, touch assistive technology, or OS zoom session was exercised.
The 200-node and 320-edge Architecture accessibility tree remains intrinsically dense and needs real assistive-technology user validation.
Polite live-region structure was verified, but spoken announcement verbosity was not.
